LAW AND MOTION 302, JUN-21-2023 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF FELIPE JESUS BURGOS'S MOTION TO LIFT STAY. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS THE FOLLOWING TENTATIVE RULING: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO LIFT STAY IS GRANTED. IN MAY 2022, THE PARTIES STIPULATED TO STAY THIS CASE IN FAVOR OF A RELATED ONE IN OUR COURT. CIRCUMSTANCES IN THE RELATED CASE CHANGED WHEN ITS PLAINTIFF'S INDIVIDUAL PAGA CLAIMS WERE COMPELLED TO ARBITRATION AND HIS REPRESENTATIVE PAGA CLAIMS WERE STAYED. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: RICHARD B, ULMER JR., CLERK: R. WOODS, NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO DISMISS ENTIRE ACTION IS GRANTED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING AND MUST DROP A HARD COURTESY COPY IN DEPT. 302'S INBOX. JUDGE: RICHARD C. DARWIN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/RCD)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-30-2026 Off Calendar

Motion To Dismiss Entire Action; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Apr-28-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF FELIPE JESUS BURGOS' Motion To Dismiss Entire Action.

Plaintiff's unopposed Motion to Dismiss Entire Action is GRANTED. Plaintiff's individual claims are hereby dismissed with prejudice, and Plaintiff's class and representative claims are hereby dismissed without prejudice, resulting in dismissal of the entire action.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Richard C. Darwin, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RCD)

PLAINTIFF FELIPE JESUS BURGOS'S MOTION TO LIFT STAY
Off Calendar Jun-21-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

PLAINTIFF FELIPE JESUS BURGOS'S MOTION TO LIFT STAY.

Appearances (via Zoom):

Jacquelyne VanEmmerik, Esq., MOON & YANG, APC, (213) 232-3128, for Plaintiff, Felipe Jesus Burgos.

GABRIEL M. HUEY, Esq., K&L GATES LLP, (310) 553.5000, Defendant, THE MOSSER COMPANIES, INC.

Ruling - A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts the following tentative ruling:

Plaintiff's motion to lift stay is granted. In May 2022, the parties stipulated to stay this case in favor of a related one in our court. Circumstances in the related case changed when its plaintiff's individual PAGA claims were compelled to arbitration and his representative PAGA claims were stayed.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order.

Judge: Richard B, Ulmer Jr., Clerk: R. Woods, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Plaintiff Felipe Jesus Burgos' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Lift Stay; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities

The Court will be continuing this matter to a date to be heard by Judge Ulmer.
